world.title: Trump ties, friction with EU: What led to Hungary strongman Viktor Orban's defeat after 16 years
world.summary: Viktor Orban's 16-year rule ended due to a combination of factors, including deepened ties with Donald Trump, China, and Russia, which led to friction with the European Union. Experts note that his loss resulted from a gradual erosion of authority, increasing public discontent, and the rise of a strong challenger within his political system.

world.title: image of himself as Christ after slamming Pope Leo as 'weak', 'terrible'
world.summary: US President Donald Trump posted an AI-generated image of himself resembling Jesus Christ on Truth Social. This action coincided with his criticism of Pope Leo XIV, whom he labeled as “weak” regarding crime and foreign policy. The image depicts Trump in robes, surrounded by light and supporters, and appears to show him 'healing' a man.

world.title: A war he opposed, a deal he couldn't secure: JD Vance's Pakistan setback
world.summary: US Vice President JD Vance's diplomatic mission to Pakistan concluded without a breakthrough amid rising tensions. Vance, known for his anti-war stance, faced challenges in securing a deal during his brief 21-hour visit. The trip highlighted the complexities of U.S.-Pakistan relations and Vance's struggle to navigate a conflict he had publicly opposed, as reported by the New York Times.

business.title: 2026 Bajaj Pulsar 180 vs Apache RTR 180 - Specs, Features, Performance Compared
business.summary: Bajaj Auto has re-launched the Pulsar 180, entering the competitive 180cc premium sporty commuter segment, previously dominated by TVS Motor's Apache RTR 180 and Hornet 2.0. A comparison of specifications, features, and performance between the Bajaj Pulsar 180 and Apache RTR 180 reveals insights into their capabilities, highlighting the ongoing rivalry in this motorcycle category.

business.title: Ola Electric shares fall nearly 8% after sharp rally; stock had surged 79% in 7 sessions
business.summary: Ola Electric shares fell nearly 8% following a significant rally, where the stock surged 79% over seven sessions. This decline is attributed to a correction after a rapid increase in share price, suggesting that the gains were driven by short-term momentum buying. The trading volume during this period indicates heightened investor activity and interest in the stock.

business.title: Delhi EV Policy Draft - Petrol Scooters, Motorcycles Ban From 2028
business.summary: The Delhi government has unveiled a draft EV Policy for 2026–2030, aiming to promote electric mobility. Starting January 1, 2027, new registrations will be limited to electric three-wheelers. Additionally, all new government vehicle purchases will be electric. The policy includes phased restrictions on petrol and diesel vehicles, with a ban on petrol scooters and motorcycles set for 2028.

technology.title: OPPO Find X9 Ultra's complete design, colour options revealed: Watch video
technology.summary: The design and color options of the OPPO Find X9 Ultra have been unveiled through an official video. The video provides detailed insights into the smartphone's camera system and accessories ahead of its launch. This announcement highlights the device's aesthetic features and functionality, generating anticipation for its release. Viewers can watch the video for a comprehensive overview.

technology.title: Why is France ditching Microsoft Windows for Linux?
technology.summary: France is transitioning its government systems from Microsoft Windows to Linux, beginning with DINUM, to decrease reliance on US technology. This initiative includes replacing Microsoft Teams and migrating health data platforms, focusing on eight critical areas of digital dependency within public administration. The move aims to enhance national control over essential systems and bolster France's technological independence.

technology.title: Elon Musk to launch XChat as part of push to build WeChat
technology.summary: Elon Musk's social media platform X (formerly Twitter) announced the launch of its instant messaging app, XChat, on the Apple App Store on April 17. This initiative is part of Musk's strategy to develop a super app akin to China's WeChat. The release date for the Android version of XChat has not yet been disclosed.

sports.title: ‘Usko maar maar ke dhoya’: R Ashwin shows no sympathy for Hardik Pandya for his captaincy blunder against RCB
sports.summary: R Ashwin criticized Hardik Pandya's captaincy decision to bowl Mayank Markande against Rajat Patidar during the recent SRH vs RCB match. Ashwin noted that Patidar effectively targeted Markande, showcasing his ability as a spin hitter. The decision ultimately backfired, leading to questions about Pandya's strategic choices in the game. The incident has sparked discussions among cricket analysts.

sports.title: Jasprit Bumrah not the real concern for MI: Why Hardik Pandya is right in throwing bowlers under the bus
sports.summary: Mumbai Indians face a critical match against Punjab Kings as they seek to address ongoing bowling issues. Captain Hardik Pandya highlighted broader concerns within the team, noting their recent three-match losing streak since their initial victory against Kolkata Knight Riders. Without a quick resolution, MI risks an early end to their season, following a narrow defeat to the defending champions.

sports.title: Hardik Pandya Gives MI Teammates "2 Options" In Tense Dressing Room Address. Video
sports.summary: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya addressed his teammates in a tense dressing room after the team faced its third consecutive loss in the IPL 2026 season. Pandya expressed disappointment over the team's performance and presented two options for moving forward. The team is struggling as they prepare to face the Royal Challengers Bengaluru in their upcoming match.

health.title: Is your diet enough? Nutritionist shares when your body actually needs nutritional supplements
health.summary: Nutritional supplements can improve health by addressing dietary gaps, especially when meeting nutrient requirements through food alone is challenging. Some individuals may be susceptible to deficiencies, making supplements beneficial. Nutritionists provide guidance on when to consider supplements and offer tips for selecting the right ones based on individual needs, ensuring a balanced approach to nutrition and health.

health.title: Mosquito menace worsens as 28 fogging machines lie non
health.summary: The city's battle against mosquitoes and vector-borne diseases is compromised, as over half of its hand fogging machines are either non-functional or in need of repairs. A report from the Nagpur Municipal Corporation highlights the inadequacy of resources to combat the growing threat. This situation raises concerns about public health and the effectiveness of mosquito control measures in the area.

health.title: FACT CHECK: Does wearing a saree cause cancer?
health.summary: Medical literature acknowledges rare cases of “saree cancer,” linked to wearing a saree tightly at the waist. The friction from the drawstring may cause skin damage, potentially leading to cancer. However, these instances are isolated and not common. The claim suggests a connection between saree wear and cancer, but evidence remains limited and not widely supported.

science.title: Artemis II crew used modern photography to tell the visual story of their lunar journey - and update some classic Apollo images
science.summary: NASA's Artemis II crew utilized modern photography techniques to document their lunar journey, aiming to create a visual narrative that updates classic Apollo images. This initiative reflects the extensive collection of materials available from NASA's human spaceflight history, including documents, artifacts, and images, allowing researchers to share the stories of past space missions while connecting them to current endeavors.

science.title: Artemis II and India’s next leap in space
science.source: The Sentinel - of this Land, for its People
science.summary: Artemis II is set to be the first crewed mission of NASA's Artemis programme, marking a significant step in humanity's renewed exploration of the Moon. The mission will transport astronauts around the Moon and back, establishing a foundation for future sustained human presence beyond Earth. This initiative reflects ongoing advancements in space exploration and international collaboration in scientific endeavors.

science.title: Artemis II: Uniting America Through Space Exploration
science.summary: Artemis II's successful moon mission has reignited American interest in space exploration, inspiring future astronauts and showcasing advancements in technology. Concurrently, Russia's Soyuz-5 rocket is reportedly prepared for upcoming space projects, as confirmed by Roscosmos head Dmitry Bakanov to President Vladimir Putin. This period marks a significant moment for international space endeavors and the potential for collaboration.

entertainment.title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ Film Leak: Six Arrested, Over 300 Links Taken Down by Cyber Crime Wing
entertainment.summary: The Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested six individuals linked to the illegal online leak of Vijay's film 'Jana Nayagan.' This action follows a complaint from the film's producers and involves multiple legal provisions. In addition, over 300 links to pirated content have been removed, with authorities issuing warnings against sharing such material.

entertainment.title: Neck-To-Neck With Shah Rukh Khan's Pathaan, Now 4th Highest Bollywood Grosser Ever!
entertainment.summary: Ranveer Singh's film Dhurandhar 2 has achieved significant success, becoming the fourth highest-grossing Bollywood film ever. Released during Eid 2026, it has maintained a strong performance at the overseas box office over 25 days. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film is now positioned alongside Shah Rukh Khan's Pathaan in the rankings of top-grossing Bollywood movies.

entertainment.title: Athiya Shetty faces heat for sharing Lata Mangeshkar’s photo while paying tribute to Asha Bhosle: ‘This is pathetic’
entertainment.summary: Athiya Shetty faced backlash for sharing a photo of Lata Mangeshkar while paying tribute to Asha Bhosle, who passed away on April 12. Although Shetty deleted the post shortly after, screenshots circulated widely, leading to criticism on social media. The incident occurred during a time of national mourning, with many tributes from the film and music community.
